title: Twilio Voice Session Setup
description: Twilio WebSocket connection arrival, new session creation, and linking to the realtime voice pipeline (RealtimeRunner/RealtimeSession). Note: VoicePipeline (STT+TTS) is NOT used here; the Twilio path uses RealtimeRunner/RealtimeSession directly for OpenAI Realtime API.
activity_flow: FastAPI GET/POST /incoming-call → return TwiML Response with <Connect><Stream url='wss://{host}/media-stream'/></Connect>
Call manager.new_session(websocket) → TwilioWebSocketManager instantiates TwilioHandler(websocket) and returns it
TwilioHandler.__init__: set playback_tracker=RealtimePlaybackTracker(), audio buffers, STARTUP_BUFFER_CHUNKS (env, default=3), STARTUP_DELAY_S (env, default=0.0)
└─ DECISION: OPENAI_API_KEY present? No → raise ValueError and abort

└─ Yes → await runner.run(model_config={api_key, initial_model_settings: {model='gpt-realtime-1.5', input/output_audio_format='g711_ulaw', turn_detection=semantic_vad}}) → self.session (RealtimeSession)
└─ await session.enter() → activates OpenAI Realtime WebSocket connection
Loop: async for event in self.session:
→ send 'media' JSON to Twilio WS (streamSid, payload)
Loop: receive_text from Twilio WS → parse JSON:
DECISION event == 'start' → capture self._stream_sid from start.streamSid
DECISION buffer has data AND time since last send > 100ms?
Yes → send all startup_buffer via session.send_audio(), mark _startup_warmed=True
Termination: WebSocketDisconnect or Exception → log and exit
key_decisions: OPENAI_API_KEY present → proceed or raise ValueError
STARTUP_DELAY_S > 0 → optional sleep before loops start
STARTUP_BUFFER_CHUNKS (default 3) → warm-up phase: buffer N chunks before first send_audio to OpenAI
Twilio event type routing: connected / start / media / mark / stop
RealtimeSession event type routing: audio / audio_interrupted / audio_end
Audio buffer >= BUFFER_SIZE_BYTES → flush immediately vs. periodic flush loop fallback
key_relationships: TwilioHandler owns RealtimePlaybackTracker (for interruption accuracy in telephony)
TwilioHandler bridges Twilio WS ↔ OpenAI Realtime API bidirectionally
VoicePipeline (STT+TTS batch) is NOT used here — Realtime API handles audio natively
important_notes: Audio format: g711_ulaw (µ-law) at 8kHz — Twilio's native telephony codec; passed straight through to OpenAI
Turn detection: semantic_vad with interrupt_response=True — model manages turn boundaries
Playback tracking: RealtimePlaybackTracker.on_play_bytes() called when Twilio sends back 'mark' events, ensuring accurate interruption truncation for telephony delay
Three concurrent async tasks run simultaneously after start(); session ends when _twilio_message_loop exits
TwilioWebSocketManager.active_handlers dict exists but cleanup/close not implemented in example
RealtimeAgent has tools: get_weather(city), get_current_time() — function tools auto-executed during session
